'''Craig Huby''' (born 27 March 1986) was an English [[rugby league]] footballer. He has previously played as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Wakefield Trinity]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]], and spent 11 years with the [[Castleford Tigers]], playing in the Super League and the second tier of English rugby league. He is currently the assistant coach at [[Sheffield Eagles]].

==Background==
Huby was born in [[Pontefract]], [[West Yorkshire]], England in 1986.<ref name="HG4Mar16" />

==Playing career==
He made his début for his hometown club, [[Castleford Tigers]], against [[Leeds Rhinos]] as a 17-year-old in May 2003.<ref name="YEP20Dec13">{{cite web |title=Castleford Tigers: Outlook testament to Huby's character |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers/castleford-tigers-outlook-testament-to-huby-s-character-1-6328143 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=20 December 2013 |quote=Huby made his debut just a month after his 17th birthday, in a televised derby against Leeds Rhinos at Headingley back in 2003.}}</ref><ref name="YEP24Dec13" /> He established himself as a regular first-team player in the 2005 National League season and his good form of that season continued in the [[Super League]] 2006.<ref name="LRL1Jul19">{{cite web |title=Craig Huby : Player Stats |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/Craig-Huby/ |website=Love Rugby League |accessdate=1 July 2019}}</ref> He suffered a number of injuries in the promotion-winning season of 2007 but was a key member of the side in 2008.<ref name="LRL1Jul19"/> In May 2008, Huby signed a three-year extension to his contract,<ref name="YEP15May08">{{cite web |title=Huby signs new deal with Cas |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers/huby-signs-new-deal-with-cas-1-2181327 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=15 May 2008}}</ref> and, in March 2011, by then Castleford’s longest serving player, he signed a further three-year contract.<ref name="Ind18Mar11">{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Huby rewarded with new Cas deal |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/huby-rewarded-with-new-cas-deal-2245071.html |website=Independent |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=18 March 2011}}</ref> A fractured kneecap, suffered in a match against St Helens in April 2011, ruled him out of the game for almost a year; he made his comeback against Featherstone in April 2012.<ref name="YEP27Apr11">{{cite web |title=Castleford Tigers: Huby sidelined by a broken kneecap |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers/castleford-tigers-huby-sidelined-by-a-broken-kneecap-1-3324459 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=27 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Huby expecting more from himself after disappointing return in Cup loss |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers/huby-expecting-more-from-himself-after-disappointing-return-in-cup-loss-1-4466556 |website=The Yorkshire Post |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=20 April 2012}}</ref> He was granted a testimonial after ten years in the senior team with a game against Halifax on Boxing Day 2013.<ref name="YEP24Dec13">{{cite web |last1=Smith |first1=Peter |title=Castleford Tigers: Fans urged to back Huby testimonial |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers/castleford-tigers-fans-urged-to-back-huby-testimonial-1-6333992 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=24 December 2013}}</ref>

He played in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]],<ref name="Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall’s double stuns Castleford">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford|newspaper=The Guardian|date=23 August 2014|accessdate=13 November 2019}}</ref> two weeks after dislocating his elbow in the semifinal against the [[Widnes Vikings]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Castleford are given lift by fans and Huby |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers/castleford-are-given-lift-by-fans-and-huby-1-6799687 |website=The Yorkshire Post |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=22 August 2014}}</ref><ref name="Gua23Aug14">{{cite web |last1=Wilson |first1=Andy |title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report |newspaper=The Guardian |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=23 August 2014 |quote=...the performance of Craig Huby, the Castleford prop, in defying the excruciating pain of a recently-dislocated elbow...}}</ref>

In September 2014, Huby joined the [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a four-year contract.<ref name="BBC11Sep14">{{cite web |title=Craig Huby: Huddersfield Giants sign Castleford Tigers prop |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/29130500 |website=BBC Sport |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=11 September 2014}}</ref> He played in every game for Huddersfield during the 2015 season but suffered a broken wrist in training in December 2015 that required surgery and led to him missing the start of the 2016 season.<ref name="Guard6Jan16">{{cite web |author1=Press Association |authorlink1=Press Association |title=Huddersfield's Craig Huby to miss start of Super League with broken wrist |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/jan/06/huddersfield-craig-huby-super-league-broken-wrist |newspaper=The Guardian |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=6 January 2016}}</ref> He was allowed to leave in December 2016 and joined [[Wakefield Trinity]] on a three-year contract.<ref name="Exam3Dec16">{{cite web |last1=Booth |first1=Mel |title=Huddersfield Giants agree to let unsettled Craig Huby join Wakefield Trinity |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/huddersfield-giants-agree-unsettled-craig-12267592 |website=Examiner Live |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=3 December 2016}}</ref><ref name="Sky7Dec16">{{cite web |author1=PA Sports |title=Wakefield Trinity sign Craig Huby from Huddersfield Giants |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12208/10681186/wakefield-trinity-sign-craig-huby-from-huddersfield-giants |website=Sky Sports |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=7 December 2016}}</ref> A bad shoulder injury suffered during a match in February 2019 led to a lengthy layoff and Huby left Wakefield Trinity by mutual consent in July 2019.<ref name="BBC18Feb19">{{cite web |title=Craig Huby: Wakefield Trinity prop out for up to four months with shoulder problem |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/47283056 |website=BBC Sport |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=18 February 2019}}</ref><ref name="BBC1Jul19">{{cite web |title=Craig Huby: Wakefield Trinity part with prop by mutual consent |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/48826827 |website=BBC Sport |accessdate=1 July 2019 |date=1 July 2019}}</ref>
